# Test mcp2go edge cases
#
# This test covers edge cases and error conditions

# Test empty tool description
-- empty_tool.json --
{
  "name": "empty",
  "description": "Empty tool"
}

exec go run .. -type mcp empty_tool.json
stdout 'Generated: empty_tool.go'
exec cat empty_tool.go
stdout 'type EmptyTool interface'
# Should still have Execute method even with no schemas
stdout 'Execute\(ctx context.Context'

# Test tool with no required fields
-- optional_tool.json --
{
  "name": "optional",
  "description": "Tool with all optional fields",
  "inputSchema": {
    "type": "object",
    "properties": {
      "param1": {
        "type": "string"
      },
      "param2": {
        "type": "integer"
      }
    }
  }
}

exec go run .. optional_tool.json
stdout 'Generated: optional_tool.go'
exec cat optional_tool.go
stdout 'Param1 \*string `json:"param1,omitempty"`'
stdout 'Param2 \*int `json:"param2,omitempty"`'

# Test invalid JSON
-- invalid.json --
{
  "name": "broken"
  "missing comma"
}

! exec go run .. invalid.json
stderr 'Error parsing'

# Test missing file
! exec go run .. nonexistent.json
stderr 'Error reading file'

# Test with no arguments
! exec go run ..
stderr 'Usage:'

# Test schema with no type (should default to object)
-- no_type.json --
{
  "properties": {
    "field": {
      "type": "string"
    }
  }
}

exec go run .. no_type.json
stdout 'Generated: notype.go'
exec cat notype.go
stdout 'type NoType struct'
stdout 'Field \*string'

# Test string format types
-- formats.json --
{
  "type": "object",
  "properties": {
    "created": {
      "type": "string",
      "format": "date-time"
    },
    "email": {
      "type": "string",
      "format": "email"
    },
    "url": {
      "type": "string",
      "format": "uri"
    }
  }
}

exec go run .. formats.json
stdout 'Generated: formats.go'
exec cat formats.go
stdout 'Created \*time.Time'
stdout 'Email \*string' # Just string for now
stdout 'URL \*string'   # Just string for now

# Test hyphenated/underscored names
-- naming_test.json --
{
  "name": "user-profile",
  "description": "User profile tool",
  "inputSchema": {
    "type": "object",
    "properties": {
      "first_name": {
        "type": "string"
      },
      "last-name": {
        "type": "string"
      }
    }
  }
}

exec go run .. naming_test.json
stdout 'Generated: user-profile_tool.go'
exec cat user-profile_tool.go
stdout 'type UserProfileTool interface'
stdout 'FirstName'
stdout 'LastName'
